_comment: Engine = how to invoke a coding-agent CLI non-interactively in the repo. The runner replaces {PROMPT} with the composed role prompt. Adjust flags to match YOUR installed CLI version. 'promptVia': 'arg' passes the prompt as an argument; 'stdin' pipes it in.

claude.notes: Claude Code headless. Use --dangerously-skip-permissions instead of acceptEdits for fully unattended runs.

codex.notes: OpenAI Codex CLI, non-interactive. Older versions: 'codex exec -a never --sandbox workspace-write'.

gemini.notes: Google Gemini CLI. -y auto-approves tool calls.

aider.notes: Aider edits files directly. It won't 'print' a clean verdict, so it works best for build roles; gate roles may need manual verdict entry.

echo.notes: Test engine: echoes the prompt back. Use with --dry-run-ish testing to see composed prompts without a real CLI.
